title: Parkio Daily — May 15, 2026
teaser: Soarin' Across America previews start soon at EPCOT. Florida resident discounts return for summer. A new Muppets snack stand is under construction.

rides.name: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind
rides.note: New pre-show crowd control in place – waits may feel longer on paper but actual queue flow has improved

rides.name: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad
rides.note: Mid-morning tends to offer the shortest waits before lunch crowds arrive

rides.name: Haunted Mansion
rides.note: Walk-on or near-walk-on windows often open mid-afternoon

bignews.title: Soarin' Across America Previews Begin at EPCOT This Week
bignews.body: EPCOT's updated Soarin' attraction begins previews in the next few days before its official summer 2026 debut. Imagineering revealed new flyover destinations, an updated musical score, fresh queue mini-games, and poster art for the Soarin' Across America version that will also open at Disneyland later this summer.

parkioInsight.text: Preview periods usually draw extra crowds to The Land pavilion. Riding before 11am or after 6pm tends to keep waits manageable while still catching the updated show.

bignews.title: Florida Residents Can Now Book $65-Per-Day Summer Tickets
bignews.body: Walt Disney World released a new Florida resident ticket offer for 2-day, 3-day, and 4-day passes valid through summer and fall 2026. The discount brings daily ticket costs down to roughly $65 per day for locals seeking multi-day admission during typically slower seasons.

parkioInsight.text: Local ticket promotions often lead to moderate crowd increases on weekends and during Florida school breaks. Visiting mid-week in June or September usually offers the best balance of value and lower wait times.

topstories.title: New Muppets Snack Stand Construction Visible at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: Construction photos show progress on FØØD by Swedish Chef, a new quick-service snack stand tied to Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring The Muppets. Signage and building structure are now visible near the attraction, with no official opening date announced yet.

parkioInsight.text: Once open, the stand will likely draw Muppets fans and curious guests in its first few weeks. Stopping by during the dinner hour or late afternoon may help you skip the longest lines for novelty snacks.

topstories.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Implements New Pre-Show Crowd Control
topstories.body: Cosmic Rewind introduced updated crowd-management procedures after guests exit the pre-show rooms. The changes aim to smooth boarding group flow and reduce bottlenecks during high-volume periods, though some guests may perceive slightly longer stated wait times as a result.

parkioInsight.text: Rope-drop or virtual queue return windows in the first operating hour tend to bypass most crowd-control adjustments. Plan your Lightning Lane or boarding group for early morning if possible.

topstories.title: Disneyland Offers Limited Resort Deals Despite Early Entry Changes
topstories.body: Disneyland released a modest resort discount for May through September 2026, along with one ticket promotion. However, the savings are weaker than expected given the recent end of Early Entry benefits and ongoing summer attendance softness.

parkioInsight.text: If you're flexible on dates, holding off until late June or July may yield better package offers as Disneyland adjusts to summer crowd patterns. Booking now locks in current rates but may miss deeper discounts later.

icymi.title: Lord Henry Mystic and Albert Meet-and-Greet Coming to Hong Kong Disneyland
icymi.body: Hong Kong Disneyland revealed character costumes for Lord Henry Mystic and his monkey companion Albert, who will begin rotating meet-and-greets in Mystic Point starting May 17. The pair originates from the park's Mystic Manor attraction.

icymi.title: National Geographic Expeditions Highlights Yellowstone Winter Trips
icymi.body: Disney Parks Blog featured a story on National Geographic Expeditions' Winter Wildlife Yellowstone experience, which pairs guests with National Geographic photographers like Max Lowe for guided nature and photography journeys. The article is part of an ongoing series on Nat Geo adventure travel.

spotlight.title: Disney CFO Says Walt Disney World Cannot Increase Attendance Because Parks Are 'Filled Up'
spotlight.body: At a recent investor conference, Disney CFO Hugh Johnston stated that Walt Disney World is unable to grow attendance because the resort is already at capacity. The claim has drawn scrutiny from analysts and fans who point to empty queue space, seasonal lulls, and past statements that contradict the assertion. The comments echo previous Disney leadership language used to justify price hikes and slower attraction investment.

parkioInsight.text: Capacity statements from leadership rarely reflect real-time guest experience. Off-peak weeks in January, September, and early December still offer walk-on windows for many attractions – plan trips during these windows if you want lighter crowds.

videos.title: I Tested the WORST Rated Restaurant in Magic Kingdom- The Plaza
videos.summary: A creator visits The Plaza Restaurant on Main Street U.S.A., often cited as Magic Kingdom's lowest-rated table-service spot, to see if the reputation is deserved.
